title: Population living in urban and rural areas
subtitle: Total estimated population living in rural and urban areas.
caption:
Population living in urban and rural areas

Total estimated population living in rural and urban areas.

This line chart tracks the total estimated global population living in rural and urban areas from 10,000 BCE to 2023. The vertical axis measures population in billions, ranging from 0 to 8 billion, while the horizontal axis spans the timeline from 10,000 BCE through the year 0 to 2023. Two distinct lines represent the data: a blue area and line for the rural population and a red line for the urban population. For the vast majority of the timeline, from 10,000 BCE until approximately 1800 CE, both lines remain extremely close to the horizontal axis, indicating populations well below 1 billion. A gradual rise begins around the year 0, with the rural population reaching roughly 0.2 to 0.3 billion by 1800. From the 19th century onward, both lines curve sharply upward. The blue rural line continues to rise, crossing the 1 billion mark and reaching approximately 3 billion by 2023. The red urban line follows a similar initial trajectory but accelerates more rapidly in the 20th and 21st centuries. By 2023, the urban line surpasses the rural line, ending near the 5 billion mark. The visual gap between the two lines widens significantly in the most recent section of the chart, showing the urban population exceeding the rural population in total numbers by the end of the period.
